authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering - Measurement process
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Organization for Standardization / IEC / IEEE
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO, IEC, and IEEE jointly publish this software and systems engineering measurement-process standard, making it authoritative for defining information needs, measures, analysis results, and measurement workflow discipline in software product analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: metric request intake, measurement definition, analysis result packaging, and measurement process review
used_for: distinguishing measure selection from product-management authority
scenario constraints for ambiguous or tenant-specific metric definitions

authoritative_sources.title: Commission Guidance on Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The SEC is the U.S. securities disclosure regulator; this guidance is the authoritative public baseline for presenting KPIs and metrics with definitions, calculation methods, methodology changes, context, and disclosure controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: metric definition, narrative synthesis, disclosure-style review, and unsupported-claim refusal
used_for: refusal of unsupported benchmark, trend, or disclosure claims

authoritative_sources.title: Data on the Web Best Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: W3C publishes web standards and recommendations; this recommendation provides vendor-neutral practices for metadata, data quality, provenance, versioning, identifiers, and usage feedback relevant to analytic datasets and delivery artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source collection, metadata validation, dataset documentation, versioning, and delivery package preparation
used_for: documentation of source lineage and refresh assumptions

authoritative_sources.title: Data on the Web Best Practices: Data Quality Vocabulary
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: W3C DQV is a public vocabulary for expressing dataset quality metadata, dimensions, measurements, and annotations, supporting explicit data-quality caveats for product analytics datasets.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: data-quality assessment, caveat creation, low-confidence escalation, and exception note preparation
used_for: explicit representation of missing, stale, incomplete, or conflicting records

authoritative_sources.title: PROV-O: The PROV Ontology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: W3C PROV-O is a public recommendation for provenance entities, activities, agents, and derivations, making it authoritative for source lineage and transformation traceability in analytics outputs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source lineage mapping, derived metric traceability, reconciliation, and evidence package preparation
used_for: traceability across telemetry, warehouse, billing, entitlement, CRM, CMS, and BI records
refusal to present unsupported or untraceable metrics

authoritative_sources.title: Digital Audience-Based Measurement Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Media Rating Council publishes recognized media measurement standards; this standard defines digital audience metric practices, reporting granularity, invalid-traffic filtration, weighting, and disclaimer expectations relevant to digital media product analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: digital audience metric definition, content performance reporting, benchmark caveat review, and quality-control escalation
used_for: digital audience and content engagement terminology
invalid-traffic, reporting granularity, and disclaimer expectations

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Privacy Framework: A Tool for Improving Privacy through Enterprise Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST publishes U.S. public technical guidance; the Privacy Framework is an authoritative public framework for identifying, governing, controlling, communicating, and protecting privacy risks in data processing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source collection, privacy and confidentiality review, audience approval check, and orchestrator return
used_for: privacy-risk caveats for subscriber, account, audience, and telemetry data
refusal of unauthorized sharing or repurposing

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 provides recognized public security and privacy controls for access control, audit logging, configuration management, system integrity, and privacy handling that constrain analytic record access and sharing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: access, audit, confidentiality, system-of-record validation, and delivery review

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising and Marketing Basics
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for deceptive and unfair advertising practices; its business guidance anchors refusal of unsupported product, marketing, audience, and performance claims from analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: claim review, narrative synthesis, disclosure-risk escalation, and adjacent legal or editorial routing
used_for: refusal of unsupported product-performance and marketing claims
routing of legal or claim-substantiation authority

authoritative_sources.title: Protecting Personal Information: A Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is authoritative public consumer-protection guidance for handling personal information, limiting access, retaining only what is needed, and safely disposing of records used in analytics workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidential source collection, audience approval review, cross-client benchmark review, and restricted-data escalation
used_for: authorization, minimization, retention, and de-identification caveats

workflow_stages.name: source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant product scope, business unit, jurisdiction, audience, metric dictionary, event taxonomy, source hierarchy, systems of record, retrieval hooks, confidentiality restrictions, approval rules, and refresh state before analysis starts.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: metric definition and source hierarchy selection
workflow_stages.description: Define active user, subscriber, account, entitlement, feature adoption, retention, churn, conversion, content engagement, search, recommendation, experiment, support, release, and benchmark metrics with explicit denominators, exclusions, identity logic, timestamps, cadence, and source precedence.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: metric calculation or report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble product health, subscriber, content, funnel, experiment, release, support, and client-delivery analytics outputs with source lineage, caveats, confidence posture, action thresholds, and audience boundaries.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: source reconciliation and data-quality review
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile telemetry, warehouse, billing, entitlement, CRM, CMS, experimentation, support, project, document, delivery, and BI records without hiding stale, incomplete, confidential, approval-sensitive, or conflicting evidence.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: narrative synthesis and disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Prepare analytic narrative, caveats, confidence notes, action thresholds, and disclosure-risk language while refusing unsupported causal, benchmark, forecast, policy, editorial, legal, contractual, or public-disclosure claims.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: escalation and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Escalate or refuse when tenant facts, evidence, source hierarchy, confidentiality, content rights, client rights, professional judgment, signoff authority, or conflicting policies prevent confident reporting.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: metric dictionaries, event taxonomies, telemetry schemas, data contracts, identity-resolution notes, denominator rules, exclusion rules, and dashboard definitions

product usage events, sessions, active-user records, feature adoption events, funnel events, release records, incident notes, feature flags, experiments, cohorts, guardrail metrics, and analytics extracts
subscriber, account, seat, license, entitlement, trial, renewal, churn, reactivation, paywall, CRM, billing, and customer segment records

content assets, content metadata, rights restrictions, publication status, editorial notes, recommendation impressions, search queries, views, completion records, click-through records, and approval notes
project plans, tickets, support cases, defect queues, client files, engagement records, service deliverables, review notes, quality checks, contractual evidence, and communication logs
source-precedence rules, refresh schedules, lineage records, data-quality reports, reconciliation notes, caveat registers, disclosure-risk reviews, and approval matrices
systems_of_record: document management and engagement records repository

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
request or validate tenant facts, product scope, metric dictionary, event taxonomy, source hierarchy, confidentiality constraints, systems of record, and approval rules required for the analysis

prepare evidence-backed metric definition summaries, analytics briefs, content or subscriber performance reports, reconciliation notes, caveat notes, disclosure-risk notes, and handoff packages without overstating authority
choose source hierarchy, metric definitions, refresh assumptions, caveats, confidence posture, and action thresholds when supported by authoritative records and tenant policy
escalate: legal, privacy, security, editorial, product-management, roadmap, pricing, financial approval, contractual, client-rights, content-rights, professional-judgment, attestation, or final disclosure decisions
client-facing publication, content performance disclosure, benchmark claim, product claim, or contractual signoff without explicit approval
subscriber, account, client, audience, experiment, support, content-rights, or unreleased performance data sharing outside authorized scope
public source guidance, tenant policy, platform policy, contract terms, client instruction, content rights, or jurisdictional rules that materially conflict
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, source lineage, metric definition, denominator logic, refresh state, audience, signoff status, confidentiality, or approval authority is unresolved
convert descriptive product analytics into causal, benchmark, forecast, policy, legal, editorial, roadmap, pricing, contractual, public disclosure, or professional-judgment claims
bypass source reconciliation, quality review, confidentiality restrictions, content rights, client rights, or approval flow to produce a cleaner metric
use client, subscriber, account, audience, experiment, support, telemetry, or content performance records in cross-client benchmarks without authorization, aggregation, and approved de-identification

domain_failure_modes: active-user metrics are published using device events while the approved tenant definition requires authenticated users and bot filtering
subscriber churn appears to improve because billing, entitlement, CRM, telemetry, and BI records have different refresh times and identity logic
mobile SDK or event taxonomy changes cause retention, funnel, or feature adoption trends to be stale or incomplete
content performance metrics are sent to a client without content-rights, editorial, approval, or delivery-scope review
cross-client benchmarks expose named client, subscriber, account, experiment, support, or unreleased content performance data
descriptive pre/post release trends are overstated as causal proof, benchmark superiority, forecast certainty, policy decision, or roadmap justification
tenant-specific metric overrides silently replace public or historical baselines and create metric drift
analytics outputs imply legal, editorial, privacy, security, product-management, contractual, financial, or disclosure approval authority
data quality caveats are omitted from management or client reports because the requested narrative needs a stronger conclusion than the records support

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific metric definitions and source hierarchy
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public measurement, disclosure, data-quality, and provenance sources set the baseline, but product-specific definitions for active user, retention, churn, adoption, engagement, content completion, and benchmark comparability must come from the tenant metric dictionary and source-precedence policy at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Client, subscriber, content, and experiment confidentiality
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public privacy, security, and consumer-protection guidance establishes a conservative baseline, but client contracts, content rights, consent records, aggregation thresholds, de-identification rules, and approved audience determine whether a specific output may be shared.

The package does not guess jurisdiction-specific privacy, consumer, securities, platform, or content-rights requirements. Tenant jurisdiction, platform policy, legal review rules, and approval owners are runtime prerequisites and orchestrator-return triggers.

notes: Authoritative information product analytics records are the tenant-designated telemetry, event pipeline, data warehouse, subscription billing, entitlement, CRM, ticketing, CMS, experimentation, project, document, quality review, delivery, and BI systems constrained by ISO/IEC/IEEE, SEC, W3C, MRC, NIST, FTC, and tenant policy baselines.

The lane stops before roadmap, editorial, legal, privacy, security, contractual, financial, client-rights, content-rights, attestation, policy, and final disclosure authority even when the analytics output is operationally urgent.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack release because all tenant-specific uncertainties are represented as runtime prerequisites, refusal triggers, scenario constraints, or orchestrator return conditions.
